title: manihot palmata var. digitiformis
intro: Manihot palmata var. digitiformis, also known as the cassava or yuca, is a type of shrub native to South America, characterized by its edible roots and leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The roots and leaves of the cassava plant have been found to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and providing essential vitamins and minerals. Cassava leaves and roots can be prepared as herbal tea, powder, or fresh juice, and are also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
